_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in managing client relationships, including determining client needs/requirements, managing client expectations, and demonstrating commitment to delivering quality results.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in managing data and AI vendor relationships, including contract performance monitoring, issue escalation, renewal negotiation, and vendor consolidation planning.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-004 (AB, cosine: 0.489), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.486).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.3/10 (raw 0.429). Shared vocabulary: [managing, relationships, skill]. DCWF-only terms [client, commitment, delivering, demonstrating, determining, expectations] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, consolidation, contract, data, escalation, issue] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.893 (P=0.895, R=0.890). Balanced P/R — the 19-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in managing client relationships, including determining client needs/requi" and "Skill in managing data and AI vendor relationships, including contract performan". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.520 > STS 0.429 (gap +0.092). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [managing, relationships, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.7%. Shared tokens: [managing, relationships, skill]. DCWF-only: [client, commitment, delivering, demonstrating, determining, expectations]. WIDAI-only: [ai, consolidation, contract, data, escalation, issue].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
